Title: The knockout of Gγ subunit HvGS3 by CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing improves the lodging resistance of barley through dwarfing and stem strengthening

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type C heterotrimeric G protein gamma subunit; OSR-like domain; transmembrane domain; cysteine-rich region; VWFC domain
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lignin strengthens stem mechanical tissues and contributes to lodging resistance; gibberellin promotes internode elongation and plant height
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field and greenhouse growth; exogenous GA3 treatment in seedlings
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; T-DNA-free homozygous mutant analysis; histology; lignin quantification; transcriptome analysis; exogenous GA3 response assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout of HvGS3 reduces plant height and cell length, increases stem strength and lignin content, and improves lodging resistance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of HvGS3 in barley caused a 37.8–43.1% reduction in plant height. 2. hvgs3 showed stronger stems, increased culm lodging resistance index, more/larger vascular bundles, thicker epidermal and mechanical tissues, and 22% higher lignin content. 3. Transcriptome data indicated activation of lignin biosynthetic structural genes and strong downregulation of GA3ox, linking HvGS3 loss to increased lignification and reduced gibberellin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced grains per spike, thousand-grain weight, spike length, and grain length
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Gβ subunit (context from discussion); HvDEP1 (genetic background context)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful gene-editing target to create dwarf, lodging-resistant barley germplasm, but potential yield and grain-size penalties must be considered
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary experimental subject of the paper. HvGS3 is treated as a signaling/common gene rather than pathway enzyme or transcription factor. Golden Promise background likely has functional deficiency in DEP1, which may influence HvGS3 function.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Bioactive gibberellins such as GA1, GA3, and GA4
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 3-oxidation reactions in the late gibberellin biosynthetic pathway to produce bioactive GAs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes stem/internode elongation and plant height
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported in paper as a critical/rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in the second internode of hvgs3 relative to wild type at jointing stage
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: HvGS3 knockout background; exogenous GA3 treatment restored seedling height response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; physiological rescue/response with exogenous GA3
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA3ox transcript was strongly downregulated in hvgs3 second internodes (log2FoldChange -5.61). 2. The paper interprets this as suppression of GA biosynthesis in the mutant. 3. Exogenous GA3 increased hvgs3 seedling height, supporting GA deficiency as a cause of dwarfing.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced GA3ox expression is associated with suppressed gibberellin biosynthesis and dwarf phenotype in hvgs3
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced plant height and possibly associated reductions in grain-related traits in the hvgs3 background
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can serve as a mechanistic marker or target for tuning barley height, but excessive suppression may reduce growth and yield traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GA3ox is not directly edited; it is a downstream pathway gene implicated by transcriptome and GA rescue evidence rather than direct genetic manipulation in this paper.
